# UC-23.2.4 · Subscription Renewal and Expansion Pipeline

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Platform &middot; **Type:** Business &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We line up whose agreements end soon with how strongly people actually use the product and whether a bigger deal is already moving, so customer-facing teams chase renewals in fair order.*

---

## Description

Builds a **forward-looking renewal pipeline** from heterogeneous billing extracts after **`customer_id_map.csv`** reconciliation: computes **`days_to_renewal`** from Stripe epoch endings, Zuora **`TermEndDate`**, Salesforce **`EndDate`**, or warehouse timestamps using **`coalesce(strptime(...))`**, restricts active subscriptions that are **not** **`cancel_at_period_end`**, attaches **30-day usage intensity** (**`dc(user_id)` ÷ entitled seats**) from product telemetry, optionally overlays **open Renewal / Add-On opportunities**, then labels each row with **renewal urgency × usage health × expansion-deal presence** — yielding ARR-weighted cohorts (**URGENT — RENEWAL RISK**, **EXPAND CANDIDATE**, etc.) for Customer Success prioritization.

## Value

**Gross retention** anchors investor decks and valuation multiples; this pipeline exposes **which logos dollar-weight material renewals inside ninety days** before telemetry-only churn narratives arrive. Finance-aligned ARR totals defend board packs; pairing renewal proximity with **usage_pct** separates genuine rescue plays from noisy calendar churn; highlighting **expansion_pipeline_usd** advances **net retention above one hundred percent**, the hallmark of durable SaaS economics. Operationally it replaces spreadsheet round-robins with a Splunk-backed cadence owned by CS leadership.

## Implementation

(1) Land subscriptions **plus** FP&A-approved **`committed_arr`** into `index=business`, Stripe/Zuora/SFDC modular pulls or DB Connect watermarks (`last_modified`). (2) Publish **`customer_id_map.csv`** and **`subscription_arr_targets.csv`** on Search Heads. (3) Enable **`index=app_events`** HEC for **`app:feature_use`** (30-day window). (4) Save the SPL as a daily dashboard panel plus hourly clone gated to **`days_to_renewal≤30`** during fiscal closes. (5) Duplicate the worklist search, append **`| stats sum(urgent_arr_weight) AS urgent_arr_usd BY renewal_signal`**, filter **`renewal_signal`** labels beginning with **`URGENT`**, and alert when **`urgent_arr_usd`** exceeds **`urgent_arr_threshold`** (example **USD 500k**) routing **CRO + VP Customer Success**.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Heavy Forwarder or mid-tier collector permitted to store REST secrets for Stripe (`sk_live_*`), Zuora OAuth client secrets, Salesforce JWT certs, plus JDBC identities for Splunk DB Connect ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)).
- Splunk roles granting **`srchIndexesAllowed`** on **`business`**, **`app_events`**, optional **`summary`** if snapshotting CSV extracts.
- Published lookups on Search Heads: **`customer_id_map.csv`** (`system`, **`raw_id`**, **`canonical_customer_id`**) maintained nightly from CRM golden records — reconcile exactly as UC-23.2.3 describes — plus **`subscription_arr_targets.csv`** carrying **`product_tier`**, **`urgent_days`**, optional uplift coefficients.
- FP&A sign-off naming the authoritative **`committed_arr`** column for Splunk versus naive billing math — paste one paragraph into dashboard footers.
- Python 3.9+ runtime available for modular inputs when REST polling replaces warehouse-first ingestion.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**
Path **A — Stripe subscriptions via modular REST polling:** Issue restricted keys under Stripe Dashboard → Developers → API keys. Ship a Splunk modular input or standalone scheduler that performs HTTPS GET loops against `https://api.stripe.com/v1/subscriptions?limit=100&starting_after=<cursor>` — pin **`Stripe-Version: 2024-04-10`** header per Stripe compatibility guidance — paginate until JSON **`has_more`** is false; optionally ingest webhook envelopes (`customer.subscription.updated`) into the same index for intra-hour deltas. Serialize each subscription JSON payload to Splunk via HEC using **`index=business`**, **`sourcetype=stripe:subscription`**, **`_time`** parsed from **`updated`** epoch seconds.

Example **`inputs.conf`** stanza invoking a packaged Python worker (`apps/TA_stripe_subscriptions/local/inputs.conf`):

```ini
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_stripe_subscriptions/bin/stripe_subscriptions_poll.py]
interval = 900
disabled = 0
index = business
sourcetype = stripe:subscription
passAuth = splunk-system-user
```

Logrotate **`splunkd.log`** ExecProcessor errors (`requests.exceptions.HTTPError`) alongside Stripe **`429`** backoff counters.

Path **B — DB Connect warehouse replicas:** Enterprise deployments mirror Stripe Billing tables, Zuora Z-Object extracts, SAP **`VBAK/VBEP`** subscription schedules, or NetSuite **`subscriptionplan`** entities into Snowflake / BigQuery / Redshift. Use JDBC scheduled inputs (`cron_schedule = 15 */6 * * *`) selecting **`subscriptions`** plus watermark **`last_modified_ts > ?`** bound parameter — sourcetypes **`dbx:subscriptions`** / **`dbx:billing_arr`**. Alias warehouse columns (`CURRENT_PERIOD_END`, `COMMITTED_ARR`) via **`FIELDALIAS`** so SPL **`coalesce()`** clauses remain stable.

Path **C — Salesforce CPQ Subscription via Bulk API 2.0:** Follow UC-23.2.1 Connected App JWT issuance; submit **`POST /services/data/v59.0/jobs/query`** jobs selecting **`SBQQ__Subscription__c`** fields (**`SBQQ__Contract__c`**, **`SBQQ__RenewalPrice__c`**, **`SBQQ__ProductSubscriptionType__c`**) — tenant-specific SOQL differs; normalize sourcetype **`salesforce:subscription`**. Maintain **`LastModified`** incremental pulls.

Path **D — Product usage:** Emit **`app:feature_use`** JSON via HEC tokens (`token=pa_usage`) capturing **`customer_id`**, **`user_id`**, **`feature_name`**. Alternate pipelines ingest **`mixpanel:event`** / **`amplitude:event`** exports rewritten through heavy forwarders.

Lookup hygiene: nightly **`outputlookup`** automation rewriting **`customer_id_map.csv`** prevents **`join`** starvation when Salesforce merges accounts mid-quarter.

**Step 2 — Create the search and alert**
Authoritative SPL mirrors the **`spl`** field above — conceptual beats:
- **Scope** unions **`dbx:*`** warehouse snapshots with REST-derived Stripe/Zuora/Salesforce feeds so migrations swap sourcetypes without rewriting downstream dashboards.
- **Identity**: **`eval raw_id=coalesce(...)`** harmonizes CRM vs billing identifiers before **`lookup customer_id_map.csv`** emits **`canonical_customer_id`**.
- **Renewal epoch tolerance**: nested **`coalesce()`** tries Unix-digit strings (**`strptime(...,"%s")`** implicit via **`tonumber`** branch), ISO8601 with timezone offsets, ISO8601 **`Z`**, then **`substr(...,10)`** date-only fallback — bridging Stripe Unix (**`current_period_end`**), Zuora **`TermEndDate`**, Salesforce **`EndDate`**, JDBC TIMESTAMP strings.
- **Cancellation hygiene**: **`where NOT (cancel_term IN (...)`** strips **`cancel_at_period_end=true`** and **`status=canceled`** — aligns with Step 5 mitigation separating scheduled churn.
- **Usage enrichment**: **`join type=left max=0 customer_id`** subsearch aggregates **`dc(user_id)`** over **`app:feature_use`** — mirrors UC-23.2.3 **`join`** discipline so Splunk never truncates multi-thousand-account cardinality at default limits.
- **CRM enrichment**: second **`join`** sums **`Amount`** on still-open Renewal / Add-On opportunities after **`lookup`** aligns **`AccountId`** to **`canonical_customer_id`**.
- **Classification**: **`case()`** mixes **`days_to_renewal`** tiers with **`usage_pct`** thresholds informed by **`subscription_arr_targets`** lookup outputs.
- **Schedule**: cron **`0 6 * * *`** localized CS timezone daily plus **`0 */4 * * *`** hourly duplicate gated **`where days_to_renewal<=30`** inside fifteen-day fiscal-close windows.
- **Alert**: duplicate the saved search, append **`| stats sum(urgent_arr_weight) AS urgent_arr_usd sum(committed_arr_usd) AS pipeline_arr_usd BY renewal_signal`**, filter **`renewal_signal` matches URGENT**, compare **`urgent_arr_usd`** against macro **`urgent_arr_threshold`** — route actions to **`cro@`** / **`vpcustomersuccess@`** with CSV attachment.

Fleet-wide overlays sometimes prepend **`appendcols [ search ... | stats avg(usage_pct) AS fleet_usage_pct ]`** when QA wants one blended ratio before **`join`** enrichment.

**Step 3 — Validate**
Cross-check **`days_to_renewal≤90`** populations against vendor consoles: Stripe Dashboard → Billing → Subscriptions filtered **`Current period ends`** window; Zuora **`Subscriptions`** filtered **`Term End Date`**; Salesforce CPQ **`Subscriptions`** tab sorted **`Next Renewal`**.

Compare **`sum(committed_arr_usd)`** versus FP&A monthly board workbook — variance triggers usually trace **discount objects**, migrated **`coupon`** stacks, or **`balance_transaction`** offsets omitted from naive SPL.

Random sample five **`URGENT`** rows: verify **`renewal_epoch`** equals portal timestamps plus reconcile **`usage_pct`** manually (**`distinct user emails`** ÷ Salesforce **`Seat_Count__c`**).

**Step 4 — Operationalize**
Dashboard shell (**Dashboard Studio** recommended):
- Row one — KPI tiles (**ARR next 30**, **ARR next 60**, **ARR next 90**, **`URGENT`**-only ARR).
- Row two — heatmap (**`renewal_signal`** vs **`usage_pct`** bucket).
- Row three — sortable table per **`customer_id`** with drilldown linking Stripe **`subscription_id`** (`https://dashboard.stripe.com/subscriptions/<id>` pattern) or internal CRM URLs.
- Row four — monthly **`timechart`** renewal ARR stacked by **`renewal_signal`**.
- Supplement **`scheduled termination`** panel sourcing **`cancel_at_period_end=true`** for salvage outreach.

Cadence: daily CS stand-up references urgent tiles; weekly **CRO + CFO** renewal pipeline touches **`timechart`**; monthly board rehearsal snapshots PNG exports.

Owner: **VP Customer Success** — escalation matrix documented in Confluence linking Splunk dashboard URLs plus **`lookup`** stewardship roster.

**Step 5 — Troubleshoot**
- **Stripe auto-renew roll-forward masking urgency** — Stripe advances **`current_period_end`** immediately after successful billing cycles while **`subscription_id`** stays constant; dashboards counting only imminent horizons lose visibility once timestamps leap seven to thirty days forward even though lifecycle intent unchanged; simultaneously **`cancel_at_period_end=true`** keeps **`current_period_end`** inside guardrails despite impending churn — **`URGENT`** cohorts inflate falsely.

**Triage SPL:** `index=business sourcetype="stripe:subscription" | stats latest(current_period_end) latest(cancel_at_period_end) latest(canceled_at) BY subscription_key | eval cancel_term=lower(tostring(cancel_at_period_end)) | where cancel_term="true" OR isnotnull(canceled_at)` — reconcile membership against salvage workflows.

**Mitigation:** Main renewal SPL retains **`where NOT cancel_term IN (...)`**; mirror dash **`scheduled churn`** listing **`cancel_at_period_end=true`** ARR separately.

- **ARR definition drift between billing APIs and FP&A committed figures** — Stripe **`items.price.unit_amount`** × **`quantity`** yields billed cadences ignoring ramps; FP&A **`committed_arr`** annualizes multi-year spreads — naive **`eval naive_arr=unit_amount*quantity*12`** misstates amortization.

**Triage SPL:** `index=business sourcetype="stripe:subscription" | eval unit_major=if(tonumber(unit_amount)>1000000, tonumber(unit_amount)/100.0, tonumber(unit_amount)) | eval naive_arr=unit_major*tonumber(quantity)*12 | stats sum(naive_arr) AS stripe_naive_sum sum(committed_arr) AS warehouse_sum BY billing_period` — divergence spikes spotlight definitional drift.

**Mitigation:** Standardize Splunk on warehouse column **`committed_arr`** landed via **`dbx:billing_arr`** — forbid improvised arithmetic for investor-facing totals unless Legal stamps methodology notes.

- **Zuora / Salesforce amendment collisions vs naive renewal horizons** — Zuora **`Subscription`** amendments spawn **`SubscriptionVersion`** duplicates sharing **`OriginalSubscriptionId`** — **`stats latest(TermEndDate)`** collapses materially different line renewals; Salesforce CPQ **`Co-Term Date`** often differs from component **`EndDate`**.

**Triage SPL:** `index=business sourcetype="zuora:subscription" | stats dc(SubscriptionVersion) AS versions dc(TermEndDate) AS distinct_end_dates BY OriginalSubscriptionId | where distinct_end_dates>1` highlights divergence needing lineage review.

**Mitigation:** Model Splunk grain at **`OriginalSubscriptionId` + TermEndDate`** tuples or compute **`min(TermEndDate)`** upcoming event per subscription family — annotate dashboards referencing Zuora **`Subscription`** vs **`SubscriptionVersion`** joins executed upstream.

Secondary monitors: webhook ingestion gaps (**`_internal`** **`HttpInputDataHandler`**), JDBC **`Communications link failure`** (`splunk_app_db_connect.log`), **`lookup`** stale merges (**`duplicate canonical_customer_id`** QA).


## SPL

```spl
index=business (sourcetype="dbx:subscriptions" OR sourcetype="stripe:subscription" OR sourcetype="zuora:subscription" OR sourcetype="salesforce:subscription") earliest=-24h@h latest=now()
| eval raw_id=coalesce(customer_id, account_id, customer, sf_account_id, crm_account_id, billing_customer_id, "")
| lookup customer_id_map.csv raw_id OUTPUTNEW canonical_customer_id AS mapped_customer_id
| eval customer_id=coalesce(mapped_customer_id, raw_id)
| eval subscription_key=coalesce(subscription_id, sub_id, contract_id, Id, SubscriptionNumber, "")
| eval cancel_term=lower(tostring(coalesce(cancel_at_period_end, cancelAtPeriodEnd, "false")))
| where NOT (cancel_term IN ("true","1") OR isnotnull(canceled_at) OR status IN ("canceled","cancelled","inactive"))
| eval renewal_raw=tostring(coalesce(renewal_date, current_period_end, TermEndDate, contract_end_date, EndDate, ""))
| eval renewal_epoch=coalesce(if(match(renewal_raw,"^[0-9]{10,13}$"), tonumber(renewal_raw), null()), strptime(renewal_raw, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"), strptime(renewal_raw, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(renewal_raw, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N%z"), strptime(substr(renewal_raw,1,10), "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval days_to_renewal=round((renewal_epoch-now())/86400,0)
| eval entitled_seats=tonumber(coalesce(entitled_seats, seat_quantity, quantity, licensed_seats, "1"))
| eval mrr_annual_usd=if(isnotnull(mrr), if(mrr>100000, mrr/100.0*12, mrr*12), null())
| eval committed_arr_usd=tonumber(coalesce(committed_arr, arr, annual_contract_value, mrr_annual_usd, contract_value, "0"))
| eval product_tier=coalesce(product_tier, price_nickname, rate_plan_name, sku_tier, "UNKNOWN")
| where isnotnull(renewal_epoch) AND days_to_renewal>=0 AND days_to_renewal<=90
| lookup subscription_arr_targets.csv product_tier OUTPUT tier_label urgent_days threshold_pct AS tier_threshold_pct
| join type=left max=0 customer_id [
    search index=app_events (sourcetype="app:feature_use" OR sourcetype="mixpanel:event" OR sourcetype="amplitude:event") earliest=-30d@d latest=now()
    | eval raw_id=coalesce(customer_id, account_id, distinct_id, group_id, "")
    | lookup customer_id_map.csv raw_id OUTPUTNEW canonical_customer_id AS mapped_customer_id
    | eval customer_id=coalesce(mapped_customer_id, raw_id)
    | stats dc(user_id) AS active_users BY customer_id
  ]
| fillnull value=0 active_users
| eval usage_pct=round(100*active_users/nullif(entitled_seats,0),1)
| join type=left max=0 customer_id [
    search index=business (sourcetype="salesforce:opportunity" OR sourcetype="dbx:opportunity") earliest=-180d@d latest=now()
    | eval raw_id=coalesce(AccountId, account_id, sf_account_id, customer_id, "")
    | lookup customer_id_map.csv raw_id OUTPUTNEW canonical_customer_id AS mapped_customer_id
    | eval customer_id=coalesce(mapped_customer_id, raw_id)
    | eval opp_type=lower(tostring(Type))
    | eval closed_flag=lower(tostring(coalesce(IsClosed,is_closed,"false")))
    | where closed_flag IN ("false","0","no")
    | where opp_type LIKE "%renewal%" OR opp_type LIKE "%add-on%" OR opp_type LIKE "%expansion%"
    | eval Amount=tonumber(coalesce(Amount, amount, "0"))
    | stats sum(Amount) AS expansion_pipeline_usd BY customer_id
  ]
| fillnull value=0 expansion_pipeline_usd
| eval renewal_signal=case(days_to_renewal<=30 AND usage_pct<50, "URGENT — RENEWAL RISK", days_to_renewal<=30 AND usage_pct>=50, "URGENT — RENEW", days_to_renewal<=60 AND usage_pct>=80 AND expansion_pipeline_usd<=0, "EXPAND CANDIDATE", days_to_renewal<=60 AND usage_pct>=80 AND expansion_pipeline_usd>0, "EXPAND — DEAL IN FLIGHT", days_to_renewal<=90 AND usage_pct<40, "AT RISK — WATCH", 1=1, "MONITOR")
| eval urgent_arr_weight=if(days_to_renewal<=30 AND match(renewal_signal,"URGENT"), committed_arr_usd, 0)
| sort - urgent_arr_weight days_to_renewal
| table customer_id subscription_key renewal_signal days_to_renewal committed_arr_usd usage_pct expansion_pipeline_usd product_tier urgent_arr_weight
```

## Visualization

**Single values**: ARR renewing inside **30 / 60 / 90** days; urgent-only ARR. **Heatmap**: **`renewal_signal`** × **`usage_pct`** bands. **Primary table**: **`customer_id`**, **`renewal_signal`**, **`days_to_renewal`**, **`committed_arr_usd`**, **`usage_pct`**, **`expansion_pipeline_usd`**, **`product_tier`** with drilldown to billing UI deep links. **Line chart**: trailing-six-quarter renewal ARR landing trend vs forecast from FP&A.

## Known False Positives

**Silent period rollovers** — Stripe advances **`current_period_end`** immediately after successful auto-charges, temporarily removing logos from an urgent slicer until the next horizon unless you explicitly bucket lifecycle exits.**Finance-grade ARR mismatch** — treasury-approved **`committed_arr`** rarely equals naive **`unit_amount × quantity × 12`** when ramp deals, vouchers, FX hedges, or prepaid waterfalls apply.**Zuora / CPQ amendment forks** — subscription-version splits emit distinct **`TermEndDate`** rows tied to one **`OriginalSubscriptionId`**, so aggregate dashboards can hide intra-quarter mini-renewals unless you pivot at **version × term-end**.**CRM overlay staleness** — Salesforce **`LastModifiedDate`** lag lets **`expansion_pipeline_usd`** read zero while Desk-less quoting progresses offline.**Usage troughs** — holiday weekends or fiscal-year invoice freezes shrink **`usage_pct`** without adoption loss.**Mixed tiers** — parent accounts bundle sandbox SKUs whose telemetry stays idle beside paid workloads.**Legal holds / procurement pauses** — contractual moratoriums postpone signatures yet billing dates remain visible.**Acquisition absorbs** — acquired subsidiaries remap **`customer_id`** mid-quarter producing phantom churn-expansion pairs.**Non-production tenants** — QA stacks indexed beside prod inflate faux urgency unless gated by **`environment`**.
